framing_templates.name: Selfie
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject as a selfie taken by the subject with a phone held at arm's length or on a selfie stick. Keep the subject close to the viewer and make the composition feel self-shot.

framing_templates.name: Mirror Selfie
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject as a mirror selfie, with the subject visible through a mirror and the phone or camera plausibly part of the composition.

framing_templates.name: Candid Unaware
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the scene as if the subject is not posing and is unaware of being photographed. Prefer natural body language and an off-center composition. Subject does not look at the camera. This framing changes only pose, viewpoint, and composition; do not add props, events, extra subjects, landmarks, or new actions.

framing_templates.name: Close-Up Portrait
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject in a close-up portrait composition focused on the face, expression, and nearby details.

framing_templates.name: Medium Shot
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject from roughly the waist or torso up, balancing the subject with some surrounding context.

framing_templates.name: Full Body
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the whole subject from head to toe, keeping the body clearly visible within the scene.

framing_templates.name: Wide Shot
framing_templates.instruction: Use a wide composition that shows the subject within a larger environment, with the setting clearly visible.

framing_templates.name: Extreme Wide Shot
framing_templates.instruction: Use a very wide composition where the environment dominates and the subject appears small within the scene.

framing_templates.name: Overhead View
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the scene from above, looking down at the subject and environment.

framing_templates.name: Low Angle
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject from a low angle viewpoint, viewed from below.

framing_templates.name: High Angle
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject from a high viewpoint looking downward, making the scene feel observed from above.

framing_templates.name: Three-Quarter View
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject from a three-quarter angle rather than directly front-on, showing depth and side detail.

framing_templates.name: Side View
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject primarily from the side, emphasizing profile, direction, and lateral movement.

framing_templates.name: Rear Three-Quarter View
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject from behind at an angle, showing the back and one side while preserving scene context.

framing_templates.name: Point Of View
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the scene from a first-person point of view, as if seen through someone's eyes.

framing_templates.name: Over The Shoulder
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the scene over one subject's shoulder, looking toward another subject or point of interest.

framing_templates.name: Product Centered
framing_templates.instruction: Place the main object clearly centered and readable, with composition organized around presenting it cleanly.

framing_templates.name: Symmetrical Composition
framing_templates.instruction: Use a balanced symmetrical composition with the main subject or layout centered and evenly arranged.

framing_templates.name: Rule Of Thirds
framing_templates.instruction: Place the main subject along a rule-of-thirds composition, with natural negative space and balanced visual weight.

framing_templates.name: Dynamic Action Shot
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject in motion with a dynamic composition that emphasizes action, direction, and energy.

framing_templates.name: Character Sheet
framing_templates.instruction: Frame the subject like a character blueprint or design reference, Always include front, side, and rear view. Character sheet overrides the original prompt - extract only the subject and put it on a plain background, standing upright and relaxed. Ignore anything that is not directly related to subject (such as any other objects/items in the scene.)

framing_templates.name: Point of view
framing_templates.instruction: The scene is viewed from the first-person perspective of the subject. Frame the scene such as 'first-person perspective of a woman looking down on her legs'.

framing_templates.name: CCTV Footage
framing_templates.instruction: Use a high angle far-from-subject composition. Rephrase the prompt to feel like a CCTV footage, do not just add a tag. Subjects are not aware of the camera.
